title: Afternoon Session
commentary: Selling pressure persists into the afternoon—Dow -1.0% leading losses as Middle East fears override the tame CPI print. Oil now +7.2% as crude claws back nearly two-thirds of Monday's historic plunge. Breadth worsening to 1:2 with new lows expanding to 156; defensive rotation accelerating. Bitcoin holding $70.7K, remarkably resilient given the equity carnage. Two hours to close and dip-buyers nowhere in sight.

title: Early Afternoon
commentary: Iran tensions escalating, driving aggressive risk-off rotation—Dow -0.9% leading declines with small caps getting hammered while Nasdaq -0.5% outperforms on Oracle's blowout AI cloud numbers. Oil now +6.5% as geopolitical risk premium roars back; energy the only green sector. Breadth still 2:1 negative with 154 new lows vs 70 new highs. Bitcoin stabilizing near $70.5K as crypto shrugs off equity weakness. FOMC speakers later may add fuel to this fire.

title: Midday
commentary: Selling intensifying into lunch—S&P now -0.6% as sticky inflation narrative lifts 10Y yields and hammers rate-sensitive sectors. Breadth ugly at 2:1 decliners; new lows outpacing highs 2-to-1. Oracle +5% on AI cloud momentum the lone bright spot in tech. Oil surging +5.8% on renewed Middle East tensions, erasing more of Monday's crash. Bitcoin slipping back below $70.3K as risk-off takes hold; gold miners crushed with HMY -12% post-earnings.

title: Mid-Morning
commentary: Equities stabilizing as CPI digestion continues—S&P paring losses to -0.2%, breadth improving with advancers now at 45%. Tech finding footing on AI tailwinds while energy whipsaws +4% as oil rebound accelerates. Bitcoin grinding higher to $70,685 as crypto decouples from equity weakness. Gold miners getting crushed (HMY -10%) despite metal holding gains; sector rotation favoring defensives over cyclicals. Volume picking up ahead of FOMC speakers this afternoon.

title: Morning Session
commentary: Markets trade mixed as CPI fails to clarify Fed's rate-cut timeline—S&P -0.4%, Nasdaq outperforming at -0.2% as AI names catch a bid. Oil rebounding +3.2% on fresh geopolitical jitters, unwinding some of yesterday's historic crash. Bitcoin reclaiming $70K handle (+0.5%) while breadth remains ugly with decliners swamping advancers 3:2. Defensive rotation underway with utilities and staples holding green; energy whipsawing on headline risk.

title: Market Open
commentary: Futures opening red as yesterday's oil crash aftermath stokes fresh inflation fears—CPI came in-line but traders aren't buying the soft landing narrative. S&P futures -0.3%, Nasdaq -0.4% with small caps lagging badly. Bitcoin giving back gains, sliding 1.7% to $69,350 as risk-off mood spreads to crypto. Dollar firming +0.2% as safe-haven flows return. All eyes on Friday's PCE print; until then, expect choppy price action as markets digest oil's violent repricing.

title: Market Close
commentary: Equities end near flat after wild session—S&P eked out +0.1% and Dow closed +0.2% as oil's historic 11% plunge on ceasefire hopes overshadowed tech weakness. Bitcoin continuing its stealth rally, firmly above $70K (+1.5%) while Ethereum lags at $2,037. Dollar consolidating losses (-0.5%) as traders digest the sudden pivot in geopolitical risk premium. Energy sector hemorrhaging but defense names and consumer discretionary picking up the slack. All eyes on CPI data later this week.

title: Late Session
commentary: Rally fading into the close—equities giving back gains with S&P nearly flat at +0.03% and Dow trimming to +0.2% at 47,846 after touching 48,124 earlier. Oil bouncing off session lows, now -11.3% at $84.07 after plunging as deep as $80, but the damage is done—this is the biggest single-day crude crash since 2020. Precious metals also retreating from highs with gold +1.0% at $5,193, silver +1.1%. Bitcoin holding $70K territory as crypto ignores the equity fade. One hour to the bell and traders locking in profits, wary of overnight headline risk.

title: Afternoon Session
commentary: Historic oil collapse accelerating—WTI now down 15.5% to $80.05, the largest single-day drop since 2020, as ceasefire speculation turns into full capitulation. Equities extending gains into the close: Dow +0.8% at 48,124, S&P +0.5%, Nasdaq +0.7%—the relief rally broadening. European natural gas cratering in sympathy with TTF -19.5%, signaling markets now pricing in imminent conflict resolution. Gold holding firm at +1.7% near $5,229 as physical demand stays strong despite risk-on flows. Two hours to the bell and sellers have completely abandoned energy.

title: Early Afternoon
commentary: Oil in total meltdown—WTI crashing 13% to $82.40 as ceasefire momentum becomes a rout, erasing nearly two weeks of war premium in a single session. Equities grinding higher: Dow breaks 48,000 (+0.6%), S&P +0.4%, Nasdaq +0.65% as the energy headwind flips to tailwind. Precious metals still catching flight-to-quality flows despite the de-escalation narrative: gold holds +1.7% at $5,228, silver +2.8%, platinum +3.2%. European session closed strong with DAX +2.3%, and the dollar continues sliding as risk-on dominates. This is the fastest war premium unwind since Desert Storm.
